_meta.description: Per-candidate score adjustments for documented Open Society Foundations / Soros donor-network campaign contributions. Adam stated the methodology 2026-04-29: 'there's other dark money driving politics; like the open society, etc... Soros money is a big penalty too... even bigger than AIPAC.' This file mirrors the AIPAC schema but with a heavier penalty curve.

schedule.verified_zero: +7 points (RESOLUTE bonus: zero Soros / Open Society funding)

vehicles_tracked: Democracy PAC + Democracy PAC II (Soros super-PAC vehicles)
_meta.rationale: George Soros and Alex Soros have spent hundreds of millions over the past decade backing progressive prosecutors, judges, ballot measures, and federal candidates. The funding is treated as foreign-linked dark-money support hostile to historically orthodox Christian moral order; magnitude justifies a steeper penalty curve than the AIPAC schedule.
_meta.sources_required: Every entry should cite at least one of: OpenSecrets donor search for the Soros family, FEC filings for Democracy PAC / Democracy PAC II, NYT / WaPo / Washington Examiner reporting on specific dollar figures, or a primary OSF disclosure.

alvin-bragg.note: Manhattan District Attorney; backed by Color of Change PAC (Soros-aligned) and other Soros-funded vehicles in his 2021 race. Aggregate documented support over $1M.

george-gascon.note: Former Los Angeles County District Attorney; documented top-tier Soros-funded prosecutor with multi-million-dollar career support from Democracy PAC and other Soros vehicles.

kim-foxx.note: Cook County (IL) State's Attorney 2016-2024; backed by multiple Soros-funded prosecutorial vehicles including Illinois Justice & Public Safety PAC.

larry-krasner.note: Philadelphia District Attorney; received $1.7M+ from Soros vehicles for his 2017 and 2021 campaigns combined.

kim-gardner.note: Former St. Louis Circuit Attorney; received $300K+ from Soros-funded Missouri Justice & Public Safety PAC across her two elected terms.

monique-worrell.note: Florida 9th Judicial Circuit State Attorney (Orange + Osceola counties). Documented Soros-funded super-PAC support during her 2020 primary and again during her 2024 reelection campaign. Suspended by Gov. DeSantis in 2023 for selective non-prosecution of violent felonies; reelected in 2024 with $200K+ from Soros-aligned vehicles.

jose-garza.note: Travis County (Austin TX) District Attorney. Received over $1.5M from Soros-funded Texas Justice & Public Safety PAC across his 2020 and 2024 campaigns. Notable selective-prosecution profile including charging an Army sergeant who shot a BLM rioter with murder.

john-creuzot.note: Dallas County (Dallas TX) District Attorney. Documented $750K+ from Soros-funded Texas Justice & Public Safety PAC across his 2018 + 2022 campaigns. Stated policy of declining to prosecute theft under $750 and certain drug felonies.

joe-gonzales.note: Bexar County (San Antonio TX) District Attorney. Documented $1.1M from Soros-funded Texas Justice & Public Safety PAC across his 2018 + 2022 campaigns. Has declined to prosecute low-level drug cases and abortion-related offenses post-Dobbs.

stephanie-n-morales.note: Portsmouth (VA) Commonwealth's Attorney. Documented Soros-funded Virginia Justice & Public Safety PAC support. Member of Fair and Just Prosecution; declines to prosecute marijuana possession and most low-level felonies.

ramin-fatehi.note: Norfolk (VA) Commonwealth's Attorney. Documented Soros-funded Virginia Justice & Public Safety PAC support during his 2021 campaign. Member of Fair and Just Prosecution; declines to seek cash bail for most defendants.

new_records._description: Soros-funded DAs not currently in scorecard but high-profile enough to warrant adding. Will be inserted by add-soros-prosecutors-v2.py with party-default baseline scores.

parisa-dehghani-tafti.name: Parisa Dehghani-Tafti
parisa-dehghani-tafti.note: Arlington County Commonwealth's Attorney. Documented over $750K from Justice & Public Safety PAC (a Soros-funded vehicle) across her 2019 + 2023 campaigns. Member of Fair and Just Prosecution.

buta-biberaj.name: Buta Biberaj
buta-biberaj.note: Loudoun County Commonwealth's Attorney 2020-2024. Defeated in 2023 by Republican Bob Anderson after declining to prosecute parental-rights cases at the height of the Loudoun County schools controversy. Received $900K+ from Justice & Public Safety PAC.

steve-descano.name: Steve Descano
steve-descano.note: Fairfax County (largest VA county) Commonwealth's Attorney. Soros's Justice & Public Safety PAC spent over $950K supporting his 2019 + 2023 campaigns. Stated policies: never seek death penalty, decline to prosecute marijuana possession, decline cash bail in most cases.

mary-moriarty.name: Mary Moriarty
mary-moriarty.note: Hennepin County (Minneapolis) Attorney. Elected 2022 with major Soros-aligned funding. Notable for declining to charge youths as adults in violent-crime cases and reducing charges on convicted murderers.

pamela-price.name: Pamela Price
pamela-price.note: Alameda County (Oakland CA) District Attorney 2023-2024. Recalled by voters in November 2024 — the second California Soros-funded DA recalled (after Chesa Boudin). Received $850K+ from Soros-aligned vehicles.

steve-mulroy.name: Steve Mulroy
steve-mulroy.note: Shelby County (Memphis TN) District Attorney. Received $750K+ from Justice & Public Safety PAC for his 2022 campaign. Has declined to seek truth-in-sentencing and announced non-prosecution policies for certain low-level offenses.

jason-williams.name: Jason Williams
jason-williams.note: New Orleans (Orleans Parish) District Attorney. Received $500K+ from Justice & Public Safety PAC for his 2020 race. Pleaded guilty in 2025 to misdemeanor tax charges; remained in office.

diana-becton.name: Diana Becton
diana-becton.note: Contra Costa County (CA, San Francisco Bay Area) District Attorney since 2017. Received $600K+ from Justice & Public Safety PAC across multiple election cycles. Member of Fair and Just Prosecution founding cohort.

mike-schmidt.name: Mike Schmidt
mike-schmidt.note: Multnomah County (Portland OR) District Attorney 2020-2024. Defeated in 2024 primary by more moderate Democrat after declining to prosecute most 2020 riot-related arrests. Received $350K+ from Justice & Public Safety PAC.
